2605.20075.title: CopT: Contrastive On-Policy Thinking with Continuous Spaces for General and Agentic Reasoning
2605.20075.paper_summary: Chain-of-thought (CoT) is a standard approach for eliciting reasoning capabilities from large language models (LLMs). However, the common CoT paradigm treats thinking as a prerequisite for answering, which can delay access to plausible answers and incur unnecessary token costs even when the model is able to identify an answer before extended thinking, a behavior known as performative reasoning. In this paper, we introduce CopT, a reformulated reasoning pipeline that reverses the usual order of thinking and answering. Instead of thinking before answering, CopT first elicits a draft answer and then invokes subsequent on-policy thinking conditioned on its own draft answer for reflection and correction. To assess whether the draft answer should be trusted, CopT recasts continuous embeddings as inference-time contrastive verifiers. Specifically, it contrasts the model's support for the same generated tokens under discrete-token inputs and continuous-embedding inputs, yielding a sequence-level reverse KL estimator for answer reliability. Our analysis shows that under certain assumptions, the expected estimate equals the mutual information between the unresolved latent state and the emitted answer token, explaining why it captures answer-relevant uncertainty rather than arbitrary uncertainty in the latent state. When the answer is deemed insufficiently reliable, CopT performs further on-policy thinking, where a second KL estimator dynamically controls draft-answer visibility, preserving useful partial information while reducing the risk of being misled by unreliable content. Across mathematics, coding, and agentic reasoning tasks, CopT improves peak accuracy by up to 23% and reduces token usage by up to 57% at comparable or higher accuracy, without any additional training. The code is available at https://github.com/sdc17/CopT.
2605.20075.paper_summary_zh: Chain-of-thought (CoT) 是一種標準方法，用於從大型語言模型 (LLMs) 中引出推理能力。<lb/>然而，常見的 CoT 範式將思考視為回答的前提，這可能會延遲獲得合理答案的時間，並在模型能夠在擴展思考之前識別答案時，產生不必要的標記成本，這種行為被稱為表現性推理。<lb/>在本文中，我們介紹了 CopT，一種重新構建的推理管道，顛倒了思考和回答的通常順序。<lb/>CopT 不是在回答之前進行思考，而是首先引出一個草擬答案，然後根據其自己的草擬答案進行後續的政策內思考，以進行反思和修正。<lb/>為了評估草擬答案是否應該被信任，CopT 將連續嵌入重新表述為推理時的對比驗證器。<lb/>具體而言，它對比模型在離散標記輸入和連續嵌入輸入下對相同生成標記的支持，從而產生一個序列級的逆 KL 估計器，用於答案的可靠性。<lb/>我們的分析顯示，在某些假設下，期望估計等於未解決潛在狀態與發出答案標記之間的互信息，解釋了為什麼它捕捉到與答案相關的不確定性，而不是潛在狀態中的任意不確定性。<lb/>當答案被認為不夠可靠時，CopT 會進行進一步的政策內思考，其中第二個 KL 估計器動態控制草擬答案的可見性，保留有用的部分信息，同時減少被不可靠內容誤導的風險。<lb/>在數學、編碼和代理推理任務中，CopT 將峰值準確率提高了多達 23%，並在可比或更高的準確率下將標記使用量減少了多達 57%，而無需任何額外的訓練。<lb/>代碼可在 https://github.com/sdc17/CopT 獲得。
2605.20075.author: Dachuan Shi et.al.
2605.20075.authors: Dachuan Shi, Hanlin Zhu, Xiangchi Yuan, Wanjia Zhao, Kejing Xia, Wen Xiao, Wenke Lee
